scan_time_et: Morning recap — US session closed 4:00 PM ET (2026-07-13)

market.advice: The US session closed with the trend intact but risk-off underneath: SPY finished 749 — above its 50-day (741) and only ~1.1% off its high — with breadth healthy at 68% of names above their MA50 and the VIX still calm (17.2, ticking up from a 16.2 five-day). But renewed US-Iran escalation and a threatened Strait of Hormuz blockade hit sentiment: chipmakers sold off hard while energy caught a bid. Leadership has rotated decisively into defensives — utilities, staples, insurers, healthcare, REITs — plus transports and energy, while high-beta semis and AI names (INTC, TSM, NXPI, LRCX, ORCL) broke down. For the next session: respect the uptrend but trade the groups the tape is actually rewarding, keep semis on a short leash into any fresh Hormuz headline, and favor clean bases over anything extended.

picks.thesis: Fortinet is the strongest tech setup on the board — a new-high breakout on a leading +104% 3-month move, RSI a controlled 64, just 1.9% off its high. Cybersecurity spend is defensive within tech, exactly what's working. TradingView agrees (BUY).
strengths: New high, RSI only 64 leaves runway
risks: Tech tape is jittery on the semi selloff

picks.thesis: Northern Trust broke to a fresh 52-week high out of a tight VCP base (3 contractions, tightest 3.6%) with its RS line at the highs — a clean, un-extended leadership move in financials, the group catching the defensive bid. TV: BUY.
strengths: New high on a tight VCP base
risks: Q2 earnings in ~9 days — a catalyst either way
RSI 69 is warm but not extended

picks.thesis: MetLife printed a new high with RS at the highs and RSI a reasonable 65 — an insurance leader whose investment book earns more the longer rates stay elevated. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY, so quant, technicals and the second opinion all line up.
risks: A sharp risk-on rotation would rob defensives of the bid

picks.thesis: Principal Financial is at a new high with RSI still cool at 55 — the least-extended insurer in the leadership group, so there's real room before overbought. TV: BUY.
strengths: New high, RSI only 55 — plenty of runway

picks.thesis: Chubb is basing tightly ~1.8% off its high and TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY — a best-in-class P&C insurer, a classic risk-off ballast that's still making money on hard-market pricing. Scan and TV agree.
strengths: Tight base near highs, TV STRONG BUY

picks.thesis: Biogen is the cleanest large-cap healthcare setup — RSI just 57 and only 3.5% off its high, so it's early in the move, not chasing. Leqembi ramp plus a deep pipeline give it a fundamental spine. TV: BUY.
strengths: Leqembi Alzheimer's ramp as a catalyst

picks.thesis: CVS Health broke to a new high on a powerful +37% 3-month turnaround with RSI a healthy 65 — a defensive healthcare re-rating that TradingView independently rates STRONG BUY. Scan and TV align.
Eli Lilly is basing ~4% off its high with RSI 64 — the mega-cap GLP-1 leader (Zepbound/Mounjaro) remains the premier secular grower in healthcare. TV: BUY.
risks: Very high share price — size carefully

picks.thesis: Monster is right at a 52-week high out of a tight base — a defensive-consumer leader working precisely while the tape is nervous. TV: BUY.
strengths: Energy-drink category leader with pricing power

picks.thesis: Coca-Cola made a fresh high with RSI 68 — a textbook defensive staple catching the risk-off bid, with pricing power and a bulletproof dividend. TV: BUY.
Grainger broke out to a new high with RSI only 61 — an industrial-distribution compounder making highs without being extended, and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY. Scan and TV agree.
risks: High absolute share price — size carefully

picks.thesis: Old Dominion is a best-in-class LTL trucker that TradingView rates STRONG BUY — transports are quietly leading, and ODFL's operating ratio is the industry benchmark. Scan and TV align.
risks: RSI 72 warm — prefer a tight entry

picks.thesis: EOG Resources is the energy pick the tape is handing you — the Strait of Hormuz threat is lifting oil, and TradingView independently rates EOG STRONG BUY. A low-cost, capital-disciplined shale leader with the Hormuz risk premium at its back.
strengths: TV STRONG BUY + oil tailwind from Hormuz risk
The one group the geopolitics is helping
risks: Oil bid reverses fast if tensions de-escalate
RSI 61 fine but energy is headline-driven

picks.thesis: ONEOK is a midstream/energy-infrastructure leader that TradingView rates STRONG BUY — fee-based cash flows give it steadier footing than E&Ps while still riding the energy bid. TV confirms.
strengths: Fee-based midstream cash flows + fat yield
Energy tailwind with less oil-price beta

picks.thesis: Targa Resources made a new high with RSI 62 — a Permian-levered midstream compounder catching the energy bid without being overheated. TV: BUY.

Dominion Energy printed a new high with RSI 67, and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY — a regulated utility catching the defensive rotation, with data-center power demand as a bonus growth angle. Scan and TV agree.
strengths: Regulated-utility defensiveness + AI data-center load growth
risks: Rate-sensitive if yields spike on the oil move

picks.thesis: Entergy is a regulated utility that TradingView rates STRONG BUY, breaking out with RSI a cool 59 — Gulf-South power demand (industrial + data center) plus classic defensiveness. TV confirms.
strengths: Data-center/industrial load growth in its footprint
risks: Storm/regulatory risk in its Gulf territory

picks.thesis: American Electric Power broke out with RSI 61 — one of the largest regulated utilities in the country, a low-beta defensive leader with a large transmission capex runway. TV: BUY.
First Industrial Realty made a new high with RSI 68 — an industrial-REIT with high-quality logistics assets and pricing power on lease renewals, catching the REIT bid as rates behave. TV: BUY.
Public Storage is basing ~2% off its high with RSI a cool 52 — the least-extended REIT leader, a self-storage cash machine with a fortress balance sheet. TV: BUY.
risks: Storage demand softens in a weak-housing scenario

picks.thesis: Arista is the cleanest large-cap AI-networking setup — RSI just 54 and 3% off its high, so it's a base-not-chase entry even as the broader semi group breaks down. The AI data-center back-end buildout is a direct revenue tailwind. TV: BUY.
risks: Guilt by association if the semi selloff broadens

picks.thesis: F5 broke out on the strongest 3-month momentum in large-cap networking (+41%) with RSI a manageable 65 — application delivery and security demand is real and defensive within tech. TV: BUY.
risks: Tech-tape sensitivity on the semi selloff

picks.thesis: Apple closed right at a 52-week high with RSI 64 — a mega-cap quality anchor holding up while high-beta tech breaks, riding a services/installed-base story. TV: BUY. The most widely held name across the platform.
strengths: Quality ballast in a jittery tech tape

picks.thesis: Crocs cleared to a new high with RSI still only mid-range (58) — plenty of room before overbought, and TradingView rates it BUY. Strong cash generation and a cheap multiple back the momentum.
risks: HEYDUDE brand still the soft spot
Consumer-discretionary sensitivity if the tape turns risk-off

picks.thesis: Werner Enterprises is a trucking leader that TradingView rates STRONG BUY, riding a +39% 3-month freight-recovery move — transports are quietly leading. TV confirms the setup.
risks: RSI 73 warm — prefer a pullback entry

picks.thesis: UnitedHealth is near a high on a strong +38% 3-month recovery with RSI 62, TV BUY — but earnings land in ~3 days, so it's a WATCH, not a chase. Let the print clear before committing.
risks: Earnings in ~3 days — binary event risk
MLR/utilization headlines have whipsawed the name

picks.thesis: J.B. Hunt is a transport leader with RSI 59 and TV BUY, but earnings hit in ~3 days — a WATCH into the print. Intermodal recovery is the thesis; let the number confirm it first.
Terreno Realty made a new high and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY, but RSI 76 is extended — a WATCH for a pullback rather than a breakout chase. Pristine coastal-logistics REIT.
risks: RSI 76 — extended, wait for a pause

picks.thesis: Valero is a refiner riding the energy bid (+23% 3-mo, new high) but RSI 82 is deeply overbought — a WATCH for a pullback. The Hormuz crack-spread tailwind is real; the entry just needs to cool off.
risks: RSI 82 — very extended, do not chase
Crack spreads reverse fast on de-escalation

picks.thesis: SiriusXM broke to a new high and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY, but RSI 78 is extended after a +30% 3-month run — a WATCH for a pullback. A Buffett-favored, cash-generative subscription name that's finally turning.
risks: RSI 78 — extended, wait for a dip

picks.thesis: Progyny is the momentum outlier — +88% 3-month, new high, TV STRONG BUY — but RSI 91 is parabolic. A WATCH only: the fertility-benefits story is strong, but never chase RSI 91; wait for a base.
